faction.name: Jujutsu High (Tokyo/Kyoto)

faction_perks.special_grade: Operational autonomy. Right to refuse any mission.

faction.description: The official governing body of jujutsu sorcerers. Hierarchy-driven, politically complex.

faction.name: Curse Users' Association
benefits: No mission restrictions — any commission accepted
Access to black market cursed tools
Faction network: cursed spirits as allies
Higher pay per mission (×1.5 stipend equivalent)
restrictions: No Jujutsu High protection or backup
Hunted by Jujutsu High if flagged

faction_perks.any_rank: Deploy cursed spirits as combat allies.
faction_perks.high_rank: Access to finger bearer intel and sealed objects.

faction.description: Loose coalition of outlaws, rogue sorcerers, and curse manipulators. Power over law.

faction.name: Culling Game (Kenjaku's Arena)
benefits: Points system: kill sorcerers or add rules for points
Points can be spent to exit the colony or unlock rules
Power scaling accelerated — high XP from kills
restrictions: Cannot leave colony without spending points
No medical support unless within colony bounds
faction.description: Kenjaku's awakening ritual disguised as a death game. No rules except the ones you write.

faction.name: Independent
benefits: No restrictions on who to work for
Can switch factions later with narrative justification
Lucky draw: random event each arc
restrictions: No mission board access — must find commissions narratively
faction.description: No allegiance. Wander, survive, decide. High freedom, high risk.
